mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points in a single month signals acute labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in 70% of instances where UNRATE spikes exceed the 0.4pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal spending and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and investment demand growth, driving corporate revenue expectations higher and producing positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns (out-of-sample accuracy < 52%).

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the index of industrial production during a late-cycle expansion indicate capacity constraints and fading manufacturing momentum, prompting institutional investors to rotate out of cyclical equities and depressing forward returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases do not precede negative 20-day returns (mean forward return > 0.1% with p-value > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation falls below 1.5% year-over-year, markets price in imminent central bank accommodation, compressing term premiums and generating positive short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in 65% of observations when CPIAUCSL YoY growth drops below the 1.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between stagnant nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment reveals underlying labor market weakness that headline employment figures mask, causing earnings downgrades and negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields a negative information coefficient over rolling 3-year windows, or forward returns exceed +0.5% in triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly expands corporate profit pools and dividend capacity, establishing a persistent positive drift in equity valuations over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns show zero or negative correlation with GDPC1 growth rates across all available historical samples.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in industrial output during a post-shock recovery phase signal persistent supply-side bottlenecks that disrupt production chains, forcing immediate equity deleveraging and negative short-term returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO contractions exceeding -0.5% during recovery phases fail to predict negative 5-day returns (hit rate < 45%).
